name: United Club Infinite Card
effective_cost: Low if you actually use the United Club

verdict.text: Keep only if you fly United enough to live in the Club

earn_note: The advertised 11x on United flights bundles the 6x base MileagePlus member earn with the card's own multiplier — still the richest United-flight earn of the three cards. Earning is a side benefit here, though; the Club is the reason to hold this card.

points.note: Same dynamically-priced MileagePlus currency as the Explorer and Quest. Club Infinite adds a 1,500 PQP annual bonus and a baseline 10%+ discount on award tickets (15%+ with Premier status), but the miles themselves are worth the same wherever you earn them.

transfer_partners.highlight: MileagePlus can't be transferred out to other loyalty programs — miles earned here are locked into United.
transfer_partners.recent_changes: You can transfer INTO MileagePlus from Chase Ultimate Rewards (1:1, via a Sapphire or Ink Preferred card) and from Marriott Bonvoy — useful for topping up an award a few thousand miles short.

credits.name: Free Checked Bags
credits.subtitle: 1st + 2nd · you + 1 companion
credits.description: First and second checked bags free on United-operated flights for the primary cardholder and one companion on the same reservation (up to $440 in potential round-trip savings per Chase's marketing).
tips: Pairs with the Club access — most cardholders are already checking bags on the same trips.

credits.name: Renowned Hotels Credit
credits.subtitle: up to $200 · prepaid stays
credits.description: Statement credits on prepaid stays booked through United's Renowned Hotels portal.
tips: Requires prepaying through the Renowned Hotels portal, not a direct hotel booking.

credits.name: Rideshare Credit
credits.subtitle: up to $150/yr
credits.description: Monthly U.S. rideshare statement credit after enrollment.
tips: Enroll once, then a monthly rideshare habit captures it automatically.

credits.name: Avis/Budget Credit
credits.subtitle: up to $100 · 2 rentals
credits.description: Statement credit on up to two Avis or Budget rentals per year, alongside complimentary Avis President's Club status.
tips: Only useful if you already rent from Avis/Budget specifically.

credits.name: Instacart Credits
credits.subtitle: up to $240/yr + full membership
credits.description: Combined monthly Instacart statement credits, plus a complimentary full Instacart+ membership (not just a trial, unlike Explorer/Quest).
tips: Monthly, no rollover — needs recurring Instacart orders to capture in full.

credits.name: JSX Flight Credit
credits.subtitle: up to $200/yr
credits.description: Statement credit on bookings with JSX, a semi-private hop-on jet service in limited U.S. markets.
tips: Irrelevant unless JSX flies a route you'd actually use.

credits.name: Global Entry / TSA PreCheck / NEXUS Credit
credits.subtitle: $120 · every 4 years
credits.description: Statement credit for a Global Entry, TSA PreCheck, or NEXUS application fee, once every four years, usable by the primary or an authorized user. Shown here amortized to an annual figure.
tips: Apply for Global Entry, not just TSA PreCheck — it includes TSA PreCheck and covers the same credit.

protection_note: A real step up from Explorer/Quest on trip cancellation — $10,000/person matches CSR outright — plus a return-protection benefit neither cheaper United card gets. The 12-hour trip-delay trigger is still dated (CSR triggers at 6 hrs), and there's still no cell-phone protection.
rental_note: Primary coverage up to $60,000 — decline the counter's CDW and pay with the card. Same cap as Explorer/Quest; CSR and Venture X go higher at $75k.

status_perks.name: United Club membership
status_perks.note: Unlimited access for you and one guest to United Club locations and participating Star Alliance lounges — Chase markets this at $750+/yr on its own, more than the entire annual fee.

status_perks.name: Premier Access
status_perks.note: Priority check-in, security screening, boarding, and baggage handling on United-operated flights.

status_perks.name: Premier Qualifying Points
status_perks.note: 1,500 PQP bonus annually plus 1 PQP per $15 spent (up to 28,000/yr) — the strongest status-earning of the three United cards.

status_perks.name: IHG One Rewards Platinum Elite
status_perks.note: Automatic mid-tier IHG status — modest upgrades and bonus points, a lightweight add-on rather than a reason to hold the card.

status_perks.name: Avis President's Club
status_perks.note: Elevated Avis status — expedited pickup and upgrade availability, layered on top of the Avis/Budget rental credit.

services.name: 25% back on inflight & Club purchases
services.detail: Uncapped statement credit rebate on food, beverages, and Wi-Fi onboard United-operated flights, plus premium drink purchases at United Clubs.

services.name: Award booking discount
services.detail: Save 10% or more when booking flights with miles (15%+ for Premier members).

services.name: No foreign transaction fees
services.detail: Zero FX fees on international spend.

services.name: Visa Infinite Concierge
services.detail: 24/7 concierge for reservations, tickets, and travel arrangements, plus Visa Infinite travel and emergency assistance services.

services.name: United Club All Access path
services.detail: All Access membership (extends Club access to more airports/partners) available after reaching Premier Gold status or $50,000 in annual card spend.

additional_cards.title: Authorized users

benefits.text: Own United Club membership and lounge access
10,000 bonus miles for adding one within the first 3 months (new accounts)
Own Global Entry/TSA PreCheck credit (shared 4-year cycle)
Earns MileagePlus miles (into the primary account)

additional_cards.note: At $75/yr, an authorized user gets their own United Club access — the same lounge benefit the primary pays $695 for — which is a strong deal if a travel companion also flies United regularly. A standalone United Club membership runs several times that on its own.

timeline.text: Fee $525 → $695; added a 1,500 PQP annual bonus, TravelBank cash on the first two Avis/Budget rentals, and unlocked United Club All Access at $50,000 in annual spend.
Chase and United announced a broad refresh across the whole United card lineup — higher fees paired with a wider bundle of category credits and richer status-earning.
